title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Does ULIP provide guaranteed returns?
ULIPs or Unit Linked Insurance Plans are life insurance policies that combine the benefits of market-linked investments and insurance protection. Thus, with ULIPs, you can invest in market-linked securities and create a good corpus for your financial goals. Moreover, with the insurance coverage offered, the plan also provides a death benefit if the insured dies during the chosen term.
While there are different benefits of ULIPs, many policyholders wonder whether the returns are guaranteed or not. So, let’s understand.
The workings of a ULIP plan:
In a ULIP plan, you choose the policy tenure, premium paying tenure, premium paying frequency and the amount of premium that you want to invest.
You also choose the fund for investment. Primarily, ULIPs offer three types of funds –
Equity funds that invest primarily in equity-oriented securities and have a high risk, high return profile.
Debt funds that invest primarily in debt instruments and have a low-risk, low-return profile.
Balanced funds that invest in a combination of both equity and debt instruments. Such funds have a moderate risk and moderate return profile.
After that, the premium that you pay, net of applicable charges, is invested in the chosen funds. The funds, in turn, invest in a diversified mix of securities that are available in the financial markets. Then, as the value of the underlying securities changes depending on the market performance, the value of the portfolio changes. Since your premium is also invested in the portfolio, your fund value grows depending on the portfolio growth.
ULIP returns:
As explained earlier, the returns from ULIPs depend on the returns generated by the portfolio. The returns generated by the portfolio, in turn, depend on the changes in the value of the underlying securities. The value of the securities, again, is market-dependent and changes as per the market moves.
It can be explained like this –
Market growsSecurity value risesValue of the portfolio risesValue of invested premium rises
The opposite is true if the market falls.
For instance, say you invest in an equity fund which invests in five equity stocks. The values of each stock are as follows –
If one unit of each stock is taken, the overall value of the portfolio is Rs.10 + Rs.12 + Rs.14 + Rs.16 + Rs.20 = Rs.72
Two days later, the prices change and are as follows –
Now the overall value of the portfolio is Rs.12 + Rs.13 + Rs.15 + Rs.15 + Rs.19 = Rs.74
While some stocks suffered a fall in prices, others grew and the overall value of the portfolio grew by Rs.2 giving you a return on investment.
Are the returns guaranteed?
As mentioned earlier, ULIP funds invest in a diversified portfolio of market-linked securities. The return from these securities primarily depends on market movements.
Since the market movements cannot be ascertained exactly, the returns are not guaranteed.  You cannot say, with certainty, that you will get x% returns from ULIPs, every year, or at all times. Depending on market movements and the funds selected, you can earn very high returns or can even incur a loss.
Conclusion
Though ULIP returns are not guaranteed, you can earn good returns if you stay invested for a long-term horizon. Over the long-term period, the financial markets tend to grow and generate good returns for investors. Moreover, as you give your investment time, the volatility risk is ironed out. Your returns also get compounded, that help in enhancing the investment corpus.
So, though not guaranteed, ULIPs have the potential to generate good returns, which are also tax-efficient in nature. The returns are also inflation-adjusted to give you a real value addition to your investments. So, to enjoy the maximum possible benefits of ULIPs, opt for a long-term investment option and enjoy the NAV growing with time to create the corpus that you need for your goals.
Disclaimer:
In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.

full_pdf_text:
Does ULIP Provide Guaranteed Returns?
ULIPs or Unit Linked Insurance Plans are life insurance policies that combine the benefits of market- linked investments and life insurance protection. Thus, with ULIPs, you can invest in market-linked securities and create a corpus for your financial goals. Moreover, with the insurance coverage offered, the plan provides a death benefit if the insured dies during the policy term, provided all due premiums are paid.
While there are different benefits of ULIPs, many policyholders might wonder whether the market linked returns are guaranteed or not. So, let’s understand.
The Workings of a ULIP Plan:
In a ULIP plan, you choose the policy tenure, premium paying tenure, premium paying frequency and the amount of premium that you want to invest.
You also choose the market linked fund for investment. Primarily, ULIPs offer three types of market linked funds –
● Equity funds that invest primarily in equity-oriented securities and have a high risk, high return profile. ● Debt funds that invest primarily in debt instruments and have a low-risk, low-return profile. ● funds that invest in a combination of both equity and debt instruments. Such funds have a moderate risk and moderate return profile.
After that, the premium that you pay, net of applicable charges and taxes, is invested in the chosen funds. The funds, in turn, invest in a diversified mix of securities that are available in the financial markets. Then, as the value of the underlying securities changes depending on the market performance, the value of the portfolio changes.
ULIP Returns:
As explained earlier, the return of ULIP can be volatile as it invests in market linked securities. Hence, ULIP returns depend on the portfolio where the fund has been invested. With the market movement, the value of the portfolio also changes. Therefore, ULIP returns are not guaranteed.
For instance, say you invest in an equity fund which invests in five equity stocks. The values of each stock are as follows –
Stock A Rs.10 Stock B Rs.12 Stock C Rs.14 Stock D Rs.16 Stock E Rs.20
If one unit of each stock is taken, the overall value of the portfolio is Rs.10 + Rs.12 + Rs.14 + Rs.16 + Rs.20 = Rs.72
Two days later, the prices change and are as follows –
Stock A Rs.12 Stock B Rs.13 Stock C Rs.15
Stock D Rs.15 Stock E Rs.19
Now the overall value of the portfolio is Rs.12 + Rs.13 + Rs.15 + Rs.15 + Rs.19 = Rs.74
While some stocks suffered a fall in prices, others grew and the overall value of the portfolio grew by Rs.2 giving you a market-linked return on your investment.
Conclusion
Though ULIP returns are not guaranteed, it has the potential to outperform other asset classes (Debt, Hybrid, commodities, etc.) in long-term. Investing in equity for the long term also gives you the benefit of compounding.
Although ULIP returns are market linked and hence subject to the market risk. ULIP may offer better returns and also allows tax exemption under income tax act, subject to provisions stated therein.
Disclaimer:
In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
